page: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARM architecture. The system utilizes AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz, featuring 64 KB L1D cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server includes 22,500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps. No GPUs are present on this instance.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth tests, reaching 11,099.42 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 481.52 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is comparatively moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the average tier. Additionally, while text generation for medium and large LLM models ranks as strong, text generation for small 135M models is weak at 55.33 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density by combining 96 dedicated physical cores with a massive 22.5 TB NVMe SSD storage array. This eliminates hypervisor overhead and provides a high ratio of storage and memory bandwidth per resource unit. The server is suited for storage-intensive and database workloads, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, static web serving, and memory-bound applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 22,500 GB NVMe SSD storage array, and a 56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and top-tier database and Redis SET operations. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation represent relative performance tradeoffs, landing in the average and weak tiers respectively. This bare-metal instance provides high resource density and is suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 ARM64 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22.5 TB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-24xl is an ARM64 bare-metal server with 96 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server combining ninety-six dedicated cores with twenty-two terabytes of NVMe storage for high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and multi-threaded data processing
